**General description:** Upstairs consists of ascending steps with repeated elevation changes and concentric muscle actions. Pace is moderate with distinct lift phases. **Accelerometer patterns:** stepwise periodic peaks higher than level walking; vertical axis shows larger upward impulses; AP axis indicates forward drive; slight pauses at step transitions. **Gyroscope patterns:** distinct pitch increases during each step-up and mild roll from lateral weight shift; yaw stable except at landings or turns. **Body parts involved:** quadriceps and gluteals for elevation, ankle plantarflexors for push-off; trunk and hip abductors stabilize; arm swing assists balance.
